channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity
item.description: On its latest deployment, the Nimitz-class carrier went from slow military satellite uplinks to modern capabilities that move terabytes of data each day. The post Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Pedal Ahead founder is out as California e-bike program operator, but questions and reporting discrepancies persist
item.description: The nonprofit was the subject of a criminal investigation earlier this year and a civil probe by the Air Resources Board, according to records.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcdonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Security leaders don't want to be held personally liable for attacks
item.description: Some security workers don’t want the personal liability consequences that come with CISO and other leadership roles.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Leveraging Avaya Experience Platform to accelerate your digital banking transformation
item.description: Banks are striving for digital innovation but regulatory constraints, data security and privacy concerns, integration challenges, and the high costs of enabling change prevent 70% from achieving their transformation goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — A power cable linking Finland and Estonia under the Baltic Sea suffered an outage, prompting an investigation, Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hackers are using Russian domains to launch complex document-based phishing attacks
item.description: Report reveals surge in phishing attacks, with Remote Access Trojans, open redirects, and malicious Office documents increasingly bypassing secure gateways.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Snowy owl rescued from car grille by Minnesota woman who saved another bird hours earlier
item.description: A great gray owl and a snowy owl are being treated by experts after being rescued by a northern Minnesota woman.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Maps is deleting location history soon, so act now to save your data
item.description: Google Maps is deleting location history soon. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows you how to back it up before it's gone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: YouTube TV price hike got you down? 5 free alternatives
item.description: Was YouTube TV’s recent price increase the straw that broke the camel’s back for you? When does the cost of streaming TV stop going up? When?!! The answer is: Never. These streaming services are going to keep creeping upward in cost until we’re all shelling out $300 a month. Enough is enough.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Trump brings Silicon Valley to Washington
item.description: Silicon Valley is coming to Washington in force, as tech leaders take on prominent roles — both official and unofficial — in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: 6 ways to turn your IT help desk into a strategic asset
item.description: The IT help desk is often considered the “dungeon” department in most IT organizations: a place where those who can’t excel at anything more meaningful in IT are left to linger, or those who are just starting out in IT must prove their mettle before launching their “true” IT careers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Timeshare terror leaves retired couple $50K in the hole after being scammed
item.description: The FBI is warning timeshare owners of a telemarketing scam tied to a Mexican drug cartel. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides steps to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The greatest keyboard never sold
item.description: Even as the latest phones and wearables tout speech recognition with unprecedented accuracy and spatial computing products flirt with replacing tablets and laptops, physical keyboards remain beloved for their efficiency. Earlier this year, for example, sci-fi novelist Robert J.
item.dc:creator: Ross Rubin, Fast Company

item.title: I tried out 3 media outlets’ AI chatbots. Here’s what happened
item.description: How fitting for Time to debut an AI chatbot last week with its annual Person of the Year package. AI was almost certainly Thing of the Year, after all, even if there’s no official award for that.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers exploited mom’s fears to steal her entire life's savings
item.description: Rising elder scams: How to recognize them and protect yourself and your loved ones. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you outsmart the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The exciting new world of Redis
item.description: If you’re a developer who wants the most feature-rich, high-performance version of Redis, your choice is clear: Redis and not a fork. If you have the time and inclination to dabble in ideological debates about open source licensing, well, you might make another choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chris Christie predicts Elon Musk's influence over Trump will end
item.description: Former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie (R) predicted in a Sunday interview that tech billionaire Elon Musk’s influence over President-elect Trump will end. “When does this end?” ABC News’s Jonathan Karl asked Christie on “This Week” of Musk’s influence on Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albania's prime minister said Saturday the government will shut down the video service TikTok for one year, blaming it for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AI cameras are giving DC's air defense a major upgrade
item.description: Artificial intelligence-based cameras are giving air defense operators unprecedented capabilities in monitoring and protecting airspace.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan
item.description: Under a new plan, about one-third of CISA's staff will keep working through a shutdown. But experts are still concerned about the impact on U.S. cyber defenses. The post More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
